location.office_time_required: in-office anchor days on Monday, Tuesday, and Friday if within commuting distance of SF, NY, London, or Berlin

level_signals: Base pay range $172,000–$258,000 in SF, consistent with Senior PM
Expected to lead org-wide initiatives and own critical intersection of product, GTM, and monetization
Requires demonstrated track record designing successful pricing strategies

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You've got technical chops, and you understand how pricing intersects with product architecture and developer workflows.
You've designed successful pricing strategies for other products and can show results.
You iterate fast, validate assumptions, and bring an outcome-focused mentality.
You know how to earn trust and get things done.
A bachelor's degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics, or a similarly quantitative field is strongly preferred.
Work with product managers and engineers to design pricing models that fit how developers actually use our platform.
Rethink existing SKUs and bundles (e.g. observability, AI agents, core DX) to maximize adoption, retention, and expansion.
Lead org-wide initiatives that reshape how we monetize the platform, such as unifying our credit model.
Own the creation of assets like dynamic ROI calculators and transparent pricing guides.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You appreciate our mission, understand the needs of modern developers, and have opinions about where the market is headed.

scope.surface: Pricing across Vercel's full product portfolio (v0, Next.js, AI SDK, observability, AI agents, core DX)
scope.business_model: developer-facing cloud platform, B2B and B2D (business-to-developer), usage-based / credit model
primary_metrics_inferred: Adoption and retention of priced SKUs
core_responsibilities: Define pricing for new products and features
Repackage and optimize portfolio SKUs and bundles
Identify and own big pricing bets (e.g. unifying credit model)
Build customer-facing tools like dynamic ROI calculators and transparent pricing guides
Unblock engineering and accelerate shipping at the intersection of product, GTM, and monetization

compensation.equity_form: equity-based compensation included in competitive compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($172K–$258K SF base)
Hybrid/remote flexibility explicitly described with clear criteria
Small, fast-moving team signals ownership and impact
JD explicitly encourages applicants who don't check every box
Clear product surface and ownership area described
red_flags: Location type is ambiguous: role can be onsite (anchor days Mon/Tue/Fri) or fully remote depending on proximity to offices — effectively two different working arrangements in one posting
No visa sponsorship or work authorization information provided
Compensation outside SF adjusted for location but no other ranges provided
Years of experience not specified anywhere in JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and scope of responsibilities; not stated)
scope.team_size_estimate (inferred from 'small, fast-moving pricing team'; not a precise number)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and platform context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'fully remote' language for those beyond commuting distance)
ambiguities: Location type: JD describes hybrid anchor days (Mon/Tue/Fri) for those near SF/NY/London/Berlin, but fully remote for others — single role has two distinct working models. Treated as hybrid with remote eligibility.
Quantitative degree is described as 'strongly preferred' which is borderline hard vs. soft — classified as soft (nice-to-have severity) per JD language, but flagged here.
No visa sponsorship or work authorization information is stated anywhere in the JD.
